critique: The job description clearly outlines real AI engineering responsibilities, such as owning "end-to-end LLM product surfaces" and running "rigorous evaluations and experiments across prompts, retrieval, and agents." It specifies a clear salary range of "$180k-220k" and requires "strong Python and NLP fundamentals," indicating a legitimate, salaried engineering position with no scam signals.

The role describes clear AI/ML engineering work to "Build knowledge graph from web data". The company has "Raised $38.5MM from top-tier VCs" and a "Team of 25 from Google, Meta, Snowflake, Kaggle", indicating a legitimate company with real product development. While compensation details are not explicitly mentioned, this is a real engineering role and does not exhibit the defined scam patterns for an `ai_washed` posting.

The description clearly outlines a "Research engineer position focused on building AI to simulate the world" and mentions "models and infrastructure for creative tools," indicating substantial engineering content. The offer of "Full benefits including unlimited PTO" further confirms it as a legitimate salaried role, with no scam red flags.

This job description clearly details a real AI engineering role, focusing on building "AI-powered medical imaging solutions using deep learning across MRI, PET, and CT modalities." It also provides strong indicators of a legitimate company and product, with "Solutions deployed on 1,000+ scanners globally" and a credentialed team. There are no elements suggesting a scam, such as partnership language or vague compensation.

The job description clearly outlines real engineering work, specifically developing a "Rust-based SDK for logging, querying, visualizing multimodal data" for "physical AI including robotics and autonomous systems." While the leadlens classifier flags 'no comp', the posting itself does not state 'no salary', 'equity-only', or 'partnership opportunity', and instead describes a concrete technical role.

The job description clearly outlines real engineering work focused on "modular probabilistic models for finance and scientific research" and "uncertainty propagation." While the `leadlens` classifier flagged "no comp," the description itself does not state "no salary" or "partnership opportunity," and it provides substantial technical content, indicating a legitimate engineering role rather than an AI-washed scam.

The role is for an "Integration Engineer" and describes concrete engineering work like "Golang backend, custom database engine, server-side HTML rendering". While the description states "Uses AI extensively", this is within the context of a clear engineering role, not a sales or partnership opportunity, and the leadlens "no comp" flag is not enough to make this an "ai_washed" scam given the presence of real technical content.

This role is for a "Distributed Data Infrastructure Engineer" and clearly outlines real engineering work, such as "Build autoscaling systems for cloud database and distributed caching layer on S3" and requiring "Go, Kubernetes, distributed systems expertise." While the leadlens classifier noted "no comp," the absence of other scam signals like "partnership opportunity" or an AI title with non-AI content means this is a legitimate engineering role.

The job description clearly outlines real engineering work, specifying technologies like "Next.js and Django" and tasks such as "build end-to-end with integrations, webhooks, background jobs." It also details the application of AI by stating the role "Uses LLM APIs extensively for healthcare operations." Despite the "Founding Engineer" title and a classifier red flag on compensation, the JD itself does not state "no salary" or describe a vague product combined with equity-only pay, which are key signals for an ai_washed scam.

This job description details a legitimate AI engineering role, focusing on "raw FMCW/MIMO signal processing to deployed embedded models (ONNX, TensorRT, edge TPUs)" and building "radar + ML for smart cities, sports analytics, defense perception" for "real hardware with real customers." Although compensation is not specified, there are no explicit scam signals like "no salary" or "partnership opportunity" that would indicate an 'ai_washed' role.

This job description outlines a legitimate engineering role with specific AI/ML tasks, requiring "Deep familiarity with LLM orchestration frameworks" and focusing on "End-to-end deployment of working AI agents in production - not a demo." While compensation details are not provided, the extensive technical content indicates a real engineering position rather than an ai_washed scam.

This job description outlines clear, technical AI engineering tasks like designing and implementing Agentic AI workflows, building LLM-powered APIs, and working on RAG pipelines using specific tools like LangChain, LangGraph, and vector databases. While the compensation is not specified (flagged by LeadLens), this is a common omission in real job postings and does not negate the presence of substantial engineering work, making it a legitimate role rather than an 'ai_washed' scam.

This posting describes a real AI engineering role focused on 'Build LLM agents using LangChain,' demonstrating clear technical content. While the 'unpaid internship' and 'performance-based stipend' are significant red flags regarding compensation and exploitation, the role itself involves genuine engineering work, therefore not qualifying as 'ai_washed' under the given criteria.

This role clearly outlines a specific engineering project, requiring a "Senior LLM engineer to ship a hybrid RAG pipeline with reranking + Ragas evals" with a detailed technical stack including "Python, LangChain, pgvector, Cohere rerank, Modal deploy, Langfuse tracing." Although it's a contract position, it offers a clear hourly rate and robust engineering content, thus not meeting the criteria for an AI-washed scam.

This is a legitimate freelance project posting, not an "ai_washed" job scam. It outlines real engineering work to "build a custom GPT/Claude integration" with a "Fixed-price $2,500 USD" and clear deliverables, indicating a genuine task despite being "Project-Based" and having "No long-term commitment".

This posting explicitly states, "Not a job - it's a partnership opportunity" and "No salary - revenue share," which are classic indicators of an ai_washed scam. Furthermore, it requires "4 partners" and asks applicants to "Reply with thumbs-up emoji to apply," signaling an MLM-style, non-standard application process without any real AI engineering work described.

The posting explicitly states "No salary for first 6 months" and that it is an "Equity-only role", which are strong indicators of an ai_washed pattern. Additionally, it notes "Vague on what we're actually building - will explain on a call" and relies on "Telegram-first communication" and "viral X posts as proof of traction," all of which are classic scam signals, despite mentioning real engineering tasks.

This role offers a clear fixed salary of "CTC INR 12-18 LPA fixed + ESOPs" and details real engineering tasks like working on "India's foundational AI model stack" and requiring "Python, PyTorch fundamentals, exposure to one of (training/fine-tuning, inference optimization, evals)". Despite noted company culture concerns, it presents as a genuine salaried AI/ML engineering position, not a partnership or scam.

The job description clearly outlines a real AI engineering role focused on "Train and fine-tune foundation models" and "Manage GPU clusters, data pipelines for pretraining at scale" with specific technologies like "PyTorch + distributed training (FSDP, DeepSpeed)". While compensation is not mentioned, this alone does not classify it as an ai_washed scam because there is substantial and specific engineering content.

This posting is a classic example of 'ai_washed' because it has 'AI' in the title ('AI Customer Success Manager') but explicitly states that the 'role involves zero AI engineering' and 'No coding required'. The core responsibilities are customer success tasks like configuring prompts and dashboards, not engineering.
